edge_case_description: Normal mixed product. Mirrors P001 Smartphone baseline.
reviews_text: Review 1 (5 stars): The display on this tablet is absolutely stunning. Reading and streaming content feels premium. The stylus response is near-instant and my sketching workflow has completely changed for the better. Battery easily lasts through a full day of heavy use. | Review 2 (4 stars): Performance is snappy for multitasking and the split-screen mode works really well. Speaker quality surprised me - loud and clear for a tablet. Only downside is the front camera is mediocre for video calls. | Review 3 (4 stars): Solid upgrade from my older tablet. The display refresh rate makes scrolling and animations very smooth. The keyboard case sold separately fits perfectly. Wish the USB port supported faster data transfer though. | Review 4 (3 stars): Good device but not exceptional. The chipset handles everyday tasks fine but struggles with demanding apps and games. The storage fills up quickly and there is no microSD slot for expansion. | Review 5 (2 stars): Screen developed a faint burn-in patch after 4 months of moderate use. NovaTech support said it was outside warranty terms even though I barely used it for video. Very disappointed. | Review 6 (4 stars): Great for students. Lightweight and the battery life is genuinely impressive. The productivity apps run smoothly. Would appreciate a headphone jack - Bluetooth only is a frustrating limitation. | Review 7 (3 stars): Decent tablet at this price but the software has a few annoying bugs that have not been patched in months. The build quality is solid but the price feels slightly high compared to competitors. | Review 8 (5 stars): Best tablet I have used at this price. The display colours are vivid and accurate. Performance is fast, apps load instantly. Video calls look great on the large screen. Excellent value overall.

test_case_type: BASELINE — mixed sentiment, below average rating
edge_case_description: Normal mixed product with performance inconsistency. Mirrors P002 Laptop baseline.
reviews_text: Review 1 (4 stars): The cooling effect is noticeable in a small bedroom. Quiet operation at the low setting is genuinely impressive. Setup took under 10 minutes. However the water tank capacity is small and needs refilling twice a day in summer heat. | Review 2 (2 stars): Does not cool a room so much as slightly move warm air around. Even at max speed the temperature difference is minimal. For the price I expected far more. The energy consumption is also surprisingly high. | Review 3 (3 stars): Works decently for personal cooling if you sit directly in front of it. The oscillation covers a reasonable arc. Build quality is acceptable. The remote control feels cheap and the buttons stick. | Review 4 (5 stars): Perfect for my small home office. Keeps my desk area cool without the noise of a traditional AC unit. The timer function works reliably. Fills a very specific need and does it well. | Review 5 (1 star): Unit started leaking water from the base after two weeks. Ruined some documents on my desk. Customer service offered a partial discount on a replacement instead of a refund. Completely unacceptable. | Review 6 (4 stars): Solid build and the ice compartment is a nice touch that noticeably boosts cooling. Castors make it easy to move between rooms. Only complaint is the digital display is very dim in daylight and hard to read. | Review 7 (4 stars): Does what it claims for a single room. The sleep mode is quiet enough to run overnight. Maintenance is straightforward. Not a replacement for air conditioning but a good supplement for targeted cooling.

test_case_type: BASELINE — mostly positive, above average rating
edge_case_description: Good product with minor complaints. Mirrors P003 Headphones baseline.
reviews_text: Review 1 (5 stars): Colour accuracy on this monitor is outstanding for creative work. My photo edits now match print output far more closely. The thin bezels make dual-monitor setup clean and professional. | Review 2 (3 stars): Brightness is not high enough for a bright office environment. I work near a window and glare is a constant issue even with the brightness at maximum. Colour reproduction is otherwise excellent. | Review 3 (4 stars): Build quality is premium and the stand is very adjustable - height, tilt, swivel, all smooth. The USB hub built into the monitor is genuinely useful. Only issue is the speakers are predictably weak as with most monitors. | Review 4 (5 stars): Replaced two old monitors with this single ultrawide and my productivity has jumped. The picture in picture mode works seamlessly. Response time is fast enough for occasional gaming as well as professional work. | Review 5 (4 stars): The factory calibration is impressive out of the box. Setup was simple and the on-screen menu is intuitive. The power brick is bulky and the cable management could be better but these are minor issues. | Review 6 (2 stars): Developed a cluster of dead pixels in the bottom right corner within 3 months. NovaTech policy requires 5 or more dead pixels for a warranty replacement which is absurd. Still dealing with their support team. | Review 7 (4 stars): Excellent monitor for the price. Text rendering is sharp and eye strain during long sessions is minimal. The blue light filter works well for evening use. Wish it had a DisplayPort cable included not just HDMI. | Review 8 (4 stars): The colour gamut coverage is wide and the HDR implementation is one of the better ones in this price range. A small amount of backlight bleed in the corners but only visible on very dark content. | Review 9 (5 stars): Best monitor I have owned. Crisp, colour accurate, and the ergonomic stand means my neck no longer aches after a full day. The build feels solid and premium. Highly recommended for designers and content creators.

test_case_type: EDGE CASE — all 5-star reviews, unanimously positive
edge_case_description: Tests whether AI correctly summarises a genuinely excellent product without inventing complaints or being sycophantic. Mirrors P004 Smartwatch.
reviews_text: Review 1 (5 stars): This running tracker is the most accurate I have ever used. The GPS locks in under 10 seconds and the route mapping is precise to the metre. Heart rate monitoring during intervals matches my chest strap exactly. | Review 2 (5 stars): Completely transformed my training. The VO2 max estimates are genuinely useful and the recovery time recommendations have helped me avoid overtraining. Battery lasts 12 hours of GPS recording which covers any race distance. | Review 3 (5 stars): Lightweight, comfortable, and the band does not irritate my wrist even on long runs. The pace alerts are accurate and the haptic feedback is noticeable without being disruptive. The companion app is excellent. | Review 4 (5 stars): Switched from a much more expensive competitor and honestly prefer this. The step cadence analysis has improved my running form. Sleep tracking is detailed and actionable. Zero regrets. | Review 5 (5 stars): Brilliant product for serious runners. The structured workout feature lets me program interval sessions and the tracker guides me through each rep. Waterproof to 50 metres so I can wear it swimming too. | Review 6 (5 stars): Bought for marathon training and it has been invaluable. Elevation tracking is accurate on hilly routes. The weekly training load summary helps me balance effort and recovery. Best purchase I have made this year.

test_case_type: EDGE CASE — almost all 1-star reviews, product failure
edge_case_description: Tests whether AI honestly reflects overwhelmingly negative sentiment without softening. Mirrors P005 AirCooler.
reviews_text: Review 1 (1 star): Paper jam on the very first print job. Cleared it following the manual. Jammed again on the second print. Returned within the hour. | Review 2 (1 star): Ink cartridges are proprietary and cost nearly as much as the printer itself. After two months of light home use I have spent more on ink than the printer cost. This is a deliberate money trap. | Review 3 (1 star): Wireless connection drops every single time it goes to sleep. I have to restart the printer and reconnect from my phone before every print job without exception. Completely unreliable for an office. | Review 4 (2 stars): Print quality on photos is noticeably banded and streaky. Text documents are passable but far below what I expected. The setup process was confusing and took over an hour. | Review 5 (1 star): The automatic document feeder misfeeds every third page. Double-sided printing works roughly half the time. For an office printer this level of reliability is completely unacceptable. Requesting a full refund. | Review 6 (1 star): Drum unit warning appeared after only 200 pages printed. Replacement drum costs more than a competing printer with equivalent features. NovaTech has clearly designed this to extract money from customers. | Review 7 (1 star): Loud grinding noise started after two weeks. Opened a support ticket - was told to buy a new drum. The printer is two weeks old. Avoid this product entirely.

test_case_type: EDGE CASE — very few reviews (only 3), sparse data
edge_case_description: Tests whether AI avoids strong generalisations from very limited data. Mirrors P006 SnapCam.
reviews_text: Review 1 (5 stars): The arc design looks stunning on my desk and the light quality is exceptional. Three colour temperature modes cover every use case from focused work to relaxed evening reading. The dimming range is very wide and smooth. | Review 2 (4 stars): Build quality is solid and the weighted base means it never wobbles. USB charging port on the base is a thoughtful addition. My only complaint is the touch controls are slightly unresponsive and require a firm deliberate press. | Review 3 (2 stars): The arm joint became loose after 6 weeks and the lamp now droops to one side unless taped in place. For a premium-priced lamp this kind of structural failure this quickly is not acceptable. Otherwise the light itself is good.

test_case_type: EDGE CASE — highly conflicting reviews, same features rated oppositely
edge_case_description: Tests whether AI captures conflicting opinions rather than averaging. Mirrors P007 ErgoDesk Chair.
reviews_text: Review 1 (5 stars): The ear hooks are an absolute game changer for running. They stay perfectly in place even during sprints and the fit is the most secure I have found in any sport earphone. Highly recommend for active use. | Review 2 (1 star): The ear hooks caused pain within 20 minutes of use. The angle puts pressure on my outer ear cartilage in a way that is genuinely uncomfortable. I cannot wear these for any extended session. | Review 3 (4 stars): Sound isolation is impressive for an open-backed sport earphone. I can hear traffic for safety while still enjoying music clearly. The balance between isolation and awareness is well judged. | Review 4 (2 stars): The fit is so poor that outside noise drowns out the audio entirely. I cannot hear my music above ambient gym noise even at max volume. Completely defeats the purpose of sport earphones. | Review 5 (5 stars): Battery life is remarkable. Over 10 hours of playback on the buds plus the case gives another 30 hours. I go a full week of daily workouts without charging. | Review 6 (1 star): Battery drained from 80 percent to empty overnight in the case without any use. Within a month the buds barely last 90 minutes of playback. Battery degradation this fast is a serious defect. | Review 7 (4 stars): IPX7 waterproofing is genuine. Wore them in heavy rain and a swimming session and they work perfectly. The charging case is compact and pocketable. | Review 8 (2 stars): Water got into one bud despite IPX7 rating and the right channel audio started cutting out. Raised a warranty claim - NovaTech said water damage was not covered. Contradicts the waterproof marketing entirely.

test_case_type: EDGE CASE — hallucination trap with vague reviews and tempting fabrication opportunities
edge_case_description: Reviews are vague and emotional with few specific features. Tests whether AI fabricates technical specs not mentioned. Mirrors P008 SleepSound.
reviews_text: Review 1 (5 stars): I was not expecting much but this lamp has genuinely made my evenings calmer. Something about the light quality feels different from normal lamps. I look forward to switching it on after work. | Review 2 (4 stars): Been using it during my evening wind-down routine for two months. My sleep has been better since I started though I cannot say for certain it is only this lamp. It is pleasant and easy to use. | Review 3 (2 stars): Did nothing noticeable for me. The light is nice but so is any warm lamp. Feels overpriced for what is essentially a softly coloured bulb. Returned after three weeks. | Review 4 (4 stars): Really like the ambience it creates in my living room. Takes a few evenings to build a habit around it but once you do it becomes a useful part of winding down. Simple to operate. | Review 5 (3 stars): Fine product. Not life changing but pleasant. The light modes are calming. Hard to justify the price but I have kept it because it does improve my evening mood somewhat. | Review 6 (3 stars): Inconsistent for me. Some evenings I notice a real difference in how relaxed I feel and other evenings it seems to do nothing. Good build quality and it looks good on my shelf.
